required_header.download_date: ClinicalTrials.gov processed this data on March 2026
required_header.link_text: Link to the current ClinicalTrials.gov record.

clinical_study.brief_title: Nivolumab Maintenance After Ipilimumab/Nivolumab Induction in Stage IV BRAF-Mutant Cutaneous Melanoma with High PD-L1 Expression

brief_summary.textblock: This study evaluates nivolumab monotherapy maintenance following completion of 4 cycles of ipilimumab plus nivolumab induction in patients with Stage IV cutaneous melanoma harboring BRAF V600E mutation and high PD-L1 expression (CPS greater than or equal to 50). Patients with treated and stable brain metastases and elevated LDH are included. The primary endpoint is overall survival at 2 years.

detailed_description.textblock: Patients with Stage IV cutaneous melanoma who completed ipilimumab 3 mg/kg plus nivolumab 1 mg/kg every 3 weeks for 4 induction cycles without disease progression or unacceptable toxicity are eligible for maintenance nivolumab 480 mg every 4 weeks. Patients must have BRAF V600E-positive tumors (by IHC or NGS), high tumor mutational burden (TMB greater than or equal to 10 mutations/Mb), and PD-L1 CPS of 50 or higher. Stereotactically treated brain metastases are permitted if stable for at least 4 weeks. Immune-related adverse events that resolved to Grade 1 or less with steroid taper are not grounds for exclusion.

clinical_study.study_design: Intervention Model: Single Group Assignment, Primary Purpose: Treatment

criteria.textblock:
Inclusion Criteria:
- Histologically confirmed cutaneous melanoma, Stage IV (M1a, M1b, or M1c)
- BRAF V600E mutation confirmed by IHC or NGS
- PD-L1 CPS greater than or equal to 50 (28-8 antibody)
- Tumor mutational burden (TMB) greater than or equal to 10 mutations/Mb
- Completed 4 cycles of ipilimumab 3 mg/kg plus nivolumab 1 mg/kg induction without progression or unacceptable toxicity
- Brain metastases permitted if previously treated with stereotactic radiosurgery (SRS) and radiographically stable for at least 4 weeks
- Elevated LDH at staging permitted (does not constitute exclusion)
- Prior immune-related adverse events (irAEs) resolved to Grade 1 or less at time of enrollment
- Age 18 to 65 years
- ECOG performance status 0 or 1
- Adequate organ function: ANC greater than or equal to 1.0, platelets greater than or equal to 75, creatinine less than or equal to 1.5x ULN, AST/ALT less than or equal to 3x ULN
Exclusion Criteria:
- NRAS mutation as co-occurring primary driver
- Uveal or mucosal melanoma histology
- Active unresolved immune-related adverse events Grade 2 or higher requiring ongoing high-dose immunosuppression (prednisone greater than 10 mg/day)
- Active brain metastases (untreated or symptomatic with increasing cerebral edema)
- Leptomeningeal disease
- Active autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppressive therapy prior to cancer diagnosis
- Prior treatment with BRAF/MEK inhibitors
- Known allergy to monoclonal antibody components
- Pregnancy or lactation
